claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me how many children you're looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and wanted to check if I'm eligible.

I have one child - Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information!

To continue assessing your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I have a few more questions.

Are you responsible for Alex? This means Alex lives with you, or you contribute to the cost of looking after them at least the same as the amount of Child Benefit you'd receive.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me. I'm responsible for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for confirming that.

Next, could you tell me about your residency status? Are you living in the UK, and do you have the right to live here (for example, are you a British citizen, have settled status, or have another immigration status that allows you to access public funds)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and I have the right to live here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information.

Just one more question: Is Alex currently in full-time education or approved training? And is Alex under 16, or if they're 16-19, are they still in qualifying education or training?

Since you mentioned Alex is 8 years old, I expect they're likely in school, but I just want to confirm this for completeness.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is 8, so they're in primary school - just regular school for their age.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Let me just check the eligibility rules to make sure I provide you with accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for all the information you've provided. Based on what you've told me, I have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me summarise your situation:
- You have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old
- Alex lives with you and you are responsible for them
- You live in the UK and have the right to live here
- Alex is in primary school (which is appropriate for their age)

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why:

**For Alex (age 8):** ✅ Eligible
- Alex is under 16 years old
- You are responsible for Alex as they live with you
- You live in the UK and have the right to reside here
- Alex is in appropriate education for their age

You can proceed to make a claim for Child Benefit. Just remember that only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child, so if anyone else is currently claiming for Alex, you would need to sort that out first.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement for Child Benefit. The claimant is responsible for Alex as Alex lives with them. The claimant lives in the UK and has the right to reside here. Alex is in full-time education (primary school) as expected for their age. There are no disqualifying factors such as the child being in local authority care, receiving certain benefits, or working 24+ hours per week. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. They are responsible for a child under 16 who lives with them, they reside in the UK with the right to live here, and there are no disqualifying circumstances. The claimant should be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement for Child Benefit. The claimant is responsible for Alex as Alex lives with them. The claimant lives in the UK and has the right to reside here. Alex is in full-time education (primary school) as expected for their age. There are no disqualifying factors such as the child being in local authority care, receiving certain benefits, or working 24+ hours per week. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. They are responsible for a child under 16 who lives with them, they reside in the UK with the right to live here, and there are no disqualifying circumstances. The claimant should be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
